headlines.title: Iranian Strike Injures 12 U.S. Troops at Saudi Prince Sultan Air Base
headlines.summary: Iranian forces attacked Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, injuring 12 U.S. troops including two seriously and damaging aircraft. U.S. personnel face heightened risks amid escalating Middle East war entering second month. Officials assess further defensive measures.

framings.angle: Details two seriously wounded Americans and breach since war began February 28
Notes several troops injured two seriously plus US aircraft damage

headlines.title: Houthi Rebels Launch First Missile at Israel from Yemen
headlines.summary: Tehran-backed Houthi rebels fired their first missile at Israel from Yemen, triggering alerts across Negev region. Israeli civilians and military face new threats as regional conflict widens. IDF reports striking regime targets in Tehran.

framings.angle: Covers war entering second month with US expecting end in two weeks
Reports one killed in Tel Aviv amid Iranian missile attack and IDF strikes

headlines.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Overrun Troops and Enter Congo's Goma
headlines.summary: Rwanda-backed rebels defeated Congolese troops and entered Goma, displacing over one million residents amid gunfire and shelling. U.N. aid operations strain under influx; regional stability threatens escalation with neighbors.

headlines.title: FBI and NYPD Arrest Man Plotting to Firebomb Palestinian Activist's Home
headlines.summary: New Jersey man Alexander Heifler arrested after undercover operation to firebomb Nerdeen Kiswani's Brooklyn home. Pro-Palestinian activists face domestic threats amid global tensions. Investigation continues on JDL-linked group.

framings.angle: Describes weekslong undercover operation and activist's protest background
Identifies suspect as JDL 613 Brotherhood member fighting antisemitism

headlines.title: Indonesia Implements Social Media Ban for Children Under 16 Years Old
headlines.summary: Indonesia enforces regulation barring under-16s from platforms like YouTube TikTok Instagram to curb pornography cyberbullying scams addiction. Southeast Asia's first such ban affects millions of youth; enforcement via age verification follows.

headlines.title: Bank of America Pays $72.5 Million to Settle Epstein Accusers Lawsuit
headlines.summary: Bank of America agrees to $72.5 million settlement with Jeffrey Epstein accusers approved by U.S. District Judge Jed Rakoff. Victims gain compensation; bank's role in handling Epstein accounts draws scrutiny on financial accountability.

headlines.title: NASA SpaceX Launches Crew-10 to Retrieve Astronauts Stuck on ISS
headlines.summary: SpaceX Falcon 9 carries NASA Japan Russia astronauts from Kennedy Space Center to ISS docking Saturday. Enables return of Butch Wilmore Suni Williams after nine months; routine rotations resume amid delays.

headlines.title: UN Declares Slave Trade Gravest Crime Against Humanity in Landmark Vote
headlines.summary: UN resolution labels transatlantic slave trade gravest crime despite Western opposition energizing African Union reparations push. Ghana's president rallied support; global reckoning on historical injustices advances accountability efforts.

headlines.title: Justice Department Plans to Share Voter Data with Homeland Security
headlines.summary: DOJ seeks state voter data for sharing with DHS citizenship check tool amid controversies. U.S. voters face privacy election integrity debates; move heightens partisan tensions over fraud allegations.

headlines.title: Israel Allows Palestinians to Stream Back into Northern Gaza
headlines.previousContext: Deadlock over hostage releases broken enabling crossings on foot through military zone

headlines.title: NATO Mounts First Coordinated Response to Baltic Sea Cable Sabotage
headlines.previousContext: Another deep-sea cable severed prompting alliance's coordinated infrastructure defense
